title: Robust Russian Flour Stir-fry
description: A garlicky, fluffy stir-fry that uses a simple flour batter as the base, combined with crisp vegetables for a crowd‑pleasing Russian comfort dish.
instructions: Whisk the flour and water together in a bowl until the batter is smooth and free of lumps.
Beat in the eggs, salt, and pepper until fully incorporated.
Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Pour a ladleful of batter into the skillet, swirling to form a thin even layer.
Cook the batter until the edges lift and the bottom turns golden, then flip and cook the other side for a minute.
Transfer the cooked sheet to a cutting board, slice into wide strips, and set aside.
In the same skillet, add another tablespoon of oil and sauté the minced garlic and sliced onion until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the shredded cabbage, julienned carrot, and sliced bell pepper, stirring constantly until the vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Return the batter strips to the skillet, toss to coat them with the vegetables and sauce, and cook for an additional 2 minutes to heat through.
Serve hot, optionally topped with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of fresh dill.
